title: Helianthocereus escayachensis
intro: Helianthocereus escayachensis, also known as Escayach's pitaya or pitahaya, is a species of cactus native to the Andes Mountains in South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, reduce oxidative stress, and promote overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Helianthocereus escayachensis include alkaloids, saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Helianthocereus escayachensis is commonly prepared as a decoction, tea, or infusion, or used in the form of a topical cream or ointment to treat skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Helianthocereus escayachensis is a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. It is native to Mexico and is a part of the Helianthocereus genus. The classification of Helianthocereus escayachensis places it within the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. The order and family classification have already been mentioned, and the genus and species levels are specified. Further classification is under the infrageneric level which is subgenus, section, and series.
